refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR bundles three distinct obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate functional concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies a specific DP) and focuses on the core problem: making spatial features available in a structured, queryable form. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent verification and design of each concern.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Confirm that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions per acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface properties across the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and locate static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent active work zones and their operational constraints.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Persist and retrieve worksite spatial data in a format agreed with planning and execution stakeholders.
refined_fr.note: The refined FR is atomic at the system level; the sub-FRs are atomic functional concerns that together satisfy the original requirement and the acceptance criterion.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured spatial graph with feature attributes (geometry, classification, safety constraints); expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign zone-level feature inventories (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; maintain zone-feature mappings in a relational schema; provide zone-based query and update APIs.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a voxel-grid occupancy model with semantic labels; support incremental updates from streaming sensor data; provide spatial query (collision, proximity, traversability) and export to planning-compatible formats.

DP4: Integrate external worksite blueprints/CAD models as reference geometry; overlay real-time sensor observations to detect deviations and dynamic features; maintain a hybrid static-dynamic feature registry; reconcile conflicts via confidence scoring.

DP5: Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → feature detection (rule-based or learned classifiers) → spatial registration → conflict resolution → persistent model store; expose model via standardized query language; support versioning and audit trails for safety compliance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → feature detection (rule-based or learned classifiers) → spatial registration → conflict resolution → persistent model store; expose model via standardized query language; support versioning and audit trails for safety compliance.

potential_sources: Feature detection logic tightly coupled to specific sensor modalities (risk: hard to swap sensors or add new modalities without redesign).
Spatial registration assumes a single global coordinate frame; multi-robot or distributed sensing scenarios may require frame negotiation (risk: coupling to centralized reference).

Conflict resolution heuristics may be domain-specific (e.g., safety-critical features always override dynamic observations); embedding policy in the pipeline couples logic to worksite safety plan (risk: policy changes require code changes).
Versioning and audit trail requirements may impose tight coupling to persistent storage schema (risk: schema evolution impacts all consumers).
coupling_check.notes: DP5 is preferred because it explicitly separates concerns (ingestion, detection, registration, resolution, storage, query) and provides clear extension points. Coupling risks are manageable via abstraction layers (e.g., pluggable detectors, policy-driven conflict resolution, schema versioning). DP1 is too vague on storage/query; DP2 is overly prescriptive (zones); DP3 is representation-specific (voxels); DP4 assumes external blueprints always available.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite features are sufficiently stable during a capture cycle to allow registration without motion compensation. Risk: High-speed dynamic environments (e.g., moving personnel, vehicles) may cause registration drift. Mitigation: Temporal windowing and motion-aware registration.

Assumption: Feature detection classifiers (rule-based or learned) have acceptable false-positive/negative rates for safety-critical features. Risk: Misclassification of hazards could violate safety plan. Mitigation: Confidence thresholding, human-in-the-loop validation, and continuous retraining.

Assumption: A single global coordinate frame is sufficient for all planning/execution consumers. Risk: Multi-robot or federated systems may require local frames or frame transformations. Mitigation: Support frame hierarchies and transformations.

Assumption: Conflict resolution policy is deterministic and can be encoded in the pipeline. Risk: Edge cases or novel conflicts may require human judgment. Mitigation: Escalation to safety officer or planning authority.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are static or change infrequently. Risk: Dynamic policy updates (e.g., new hazard zones) may not propagate to the model in time. Mitigation: Real-time policy subscription and model invalidation.

Assumption: Acceptance criteria (model generation and availability) imply synchronous or near-real-time delivery. Risk: Latency in feature detection or registration may delay planning. Mitigation: Streaming updates and incremental model refinement.

Assumption: Audit trail and versioning are required for compliance but do not specify retention or query performance. Risk: Unbounded growth of audit logs may degrade query performance. Mitigation: Tiered storage and archival policies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 to explicitly decouple policy-driven conflict resolution from the core pipeline: (a) Define a conflict resolution interface that accepts feature observations and returns resolved features based on pluggable policy rules. (b) Separate safety plan and operational policy as external inputs to the conflict resolver, not embedded in the pipeline. (c) Introduce a model versioning and invalidation mechanism to support dynamic policy updates. (d) Specify the query interface contract (e.g., spatial range queries, feature classification filters, temporal snapshots) to enable independent consumer design. (e) Add a confidence/provenance metadata layer to feature records to support downstream risk assessment and human validation. This revision reduces coupling to specific policies, sensors, and storage implementations while maintaining traceability for safety compliance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized feature capture pipeline including raw sensor ingestion, feature detection, spatial registration, conflict resolution, persistent model store, standardized query interface, versioning, and audit trail management for safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Raw sensor data ingestion from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; feeds feature detection pipeline

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Audit trail and versioning metadata exported for safety compliance verification and incident reconstruction

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Query interface for downstream consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to retrieve registered features and conflict-resolved model state

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Optional: distributed model synchronization and remote audit log persistence if multi-node deployment required

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: NGHE is the natural home for perception/feature abstraction logic. Raw ingestion, detection, registration, and conflict resolution are core NGHE responsibilities. Persistent model store and query language are NGHE-owned data services. Versioning and audit trails align with NGHE's role as the authoritative feature model. Minor diffusion: SafetyAlert interface requires coordination with Safety Services for compliance reporting, but audit data originates in NGHE.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a consumer of features, not the producer/manager. Feature detection, registration, and model persistence belong at system level, not vehicle-specific. Placing DP5 here would fragment the model across multiple vehicle instances and break the unified query/versioning contract. High diffusion: conflict resolution and audit trails would need to escape to NGHE anyway.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a perception pipeline owner. Feature capture is independent of teleoperation mode. Placing DP5 here would couple perception to operator interaction, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion: raw sensor ingestion, spatial registration, and persistent model store have no natural home in Teleoperation.

Weak fit: Safety Services consumes audit trails and versioning for compliance, but does not own the feature capture pipeline itself. Feature detection, registration, and conflict resolution are not safety-specific; they are perception-layer responsibilities. Placing DP5 here would conflate safety monitoring with feature engineering. Moderate diffusion: raw ingestion, detection logic, and model store would need to be owned elsewhere.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The feature capture pipeline (DP5) is fundamentally a perception and world-model abstraction responsibility. NGHE Solution is the designated system-level perception/feature authority that owns the authoritative model of the environment. All upstream data flows (Telemetry) and downstream query consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) interact with NGHE's model via standardized interfaces. Versioning and audit trails are NGHE-owned metadata that support safety compliance without requiring Safety Services to own the pipeline itself.
containment_risks: Risk: If raw sensor ingestion is physically distributed across Vehicle or Teleoperation hardware, NGHE must establish clear data contracts (Telemetry interface) to pull/push data without tight coupling to sensor hardware specifics.

Risk: Conflict resolution logic may require domain-specific knowledge (e.g., vehicle kinematics, operator intent) that could tempt diffusion into Vehicle or Teleoperation. Mitigation: encapsulate domain knowledge as pluggable conflict resolvers within NGHE, not as external modules.

Risk: Audit trail compliance requirements may evolve; if Safety Services imposes new audit schema requirements, NGHE must absorb them without fragmenting the model store. Mitigation: define audit interface contract early with Safety Services.

Risk: Spatial registration may require real-time performance; if NGHE becomes a bottleneck, pressure may arise to push registration logic into Vehicle. Mitigation: design NGHE's query interface to support caching and lazy evaluation at consumer level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criteria explicitly require 'worksite model/map generated and available to planning/execution functions for agreed set of site features.' NGHE Solution's containment score of 0.92 and ownership of persistent model store + standardized query interface (Tasking) directly satisfy this. Verifiable via: (1) model persistence tests, (2) query interface contract validation, (3) feature availability audit against agreed feature set, (4) integration tests with Vehicle and Teleoperation consumers.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR maps unambiguously to DP5 (feature capture pipeline) and DP5 maps to NGHE Solution as primary owner. External interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link optional) establish explicit data contracts. Ownership boundary is well-defined: NGHE owns pipeline logic, model persistence, versioning, and audit metadata; Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services are consumers only.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 comprehensively addresses FR: raw sensor ingestion (Telemetry interface) → feature detection (rule-based or learned classifiers) → spatial registration → conflict resolution → persistent model store (satisfies 'machine-readable model') + standardized query language (satisfies 'available to planning/execution') + versioning and audit trails (satisfies 'safety compliance'). DP is solution-complete and logically sound. No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: Risk: Raw sensor ingestion physically distributed across Vehicle/Teleoperation hardware. Mitigation: enforce strict Telemetry interface contract; NGHE must not depend on sensor hardware specifics, only on standardized data schemas. Validate via interface compliance tests.

Risk: Conflict resolution logic may be tempted into Vehicle (e.g., vehicle kinematics) or Teleoperation (e.g., operator intent). Mitigation: encapsulate domain knowledge as pluggable conflict resolver plugins within NGHE; define resolver interface contract early. Validate via architecture review and module dependency analysis.

Risk: Audit trail schema evolution driven by Safety Services compliance requirements. Mitigation: establish audit interface contract with Safety Services early; design audit metadata schema for extensibility (e.g., key-value pairs, versioned schemas). Validate via safety compliance review and audit schema versioning tests.

Risk: Spatial registration performance bottleneck if NGHE becomes synchronous gatekeeper. Mitigation: design Tasking query interface to support caching, lazy evaluation, and asynchronous model updates at consumer level. Validate via performance testing and latency SLAs.

Risk: Multi-node deployment (Network Link interface) may introduce distributed consistency challenges (e.g., conflicting feature updates across nodes). Mitigation: define distributed consensus protocol or eventual consistency model early; document in NGHE design specification. Validate via distributed system testing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). NGHE Solution is the natural and correct home for DP5. All core responsibilities (raw ingestion, detection, registration, conflict resolution, model persistence, query interface, versioning, audit trails) are NGHE-owned. External interfaces are well-defined and minimize coupling: Telemetry (pull/push raw data), Tasking (query/retrieve features), SafetyAlert (export audit metadata), Network Link (optional distributed sync). Minor diffusion (0.08) is acceptable and managed: SafetyAlert coordination with Safety Services is a data export, not a logic ownership issue. No evidence of scattered DP responsibilities across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Ownership boundary is crisp and defensible.

recommended_revisions: Formalize Telemetry interface contract: define raw sensor data schema, ingestion rate/latency SLAs, and error handling (e.g., sensor dropout, malformed data). Ensure NGHE does not depend on Vehicle/Teleoperation hardware specifics.

Formalize Tasking query interface contract: define query language (e.g., spatial bounding box, feature type filters, temporal range), response schema, caching policy, and latency SLAs. Ensure Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services can consume features without tight coupling to NGHE internals.

Formalize SafetyAlert audit interface contract with Safety Services: define audit metadata schema (e.g., timestamp, feature ID, operation type, user/system ID, change delta), versioning strategy, and compliance reporting requirements. Ensure audit data is exportable without fragmenting model store.

Define conflict resolution plugin interface: specify how domain-specific resolvers (vehicle kinematics, operator intent, sensor fusion heuristics) are registered and invoked within NGHE. Ensure resolvers are encapsulated and do not leak into Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.

Document distributed model synchronization strategy (Network Link): if multi-node deployment is required, specify consensus protocol, eventual consistency model, conflict resolution for distributed updates, and audit trail consistency guarantees. Validate via distributed system design review.

Add performance acceptance criteria: define latency SLAs for feature detection, spatial registration, conflict resolution, and query response times. Ensure NGHE does not become a bottleneck for real-time Vehicle/Teleoperation operations.

Add versioning and audit trail acceptance criteria: specify audit trail retention policy, versioning granularity (per-feature or per-model-snapshot), and compliance reporting format. Ensure alignment with 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' constraint.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence and clear ownership. However, the recommended_revisions and diffusion_risks reveal that DP5 (feature capture pipeline) is logically sound but architecturally underspecified at the interface and distributed system levels. Given high safety criticality, the recommended revisions—particularly formalization of Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and conflict resolution plugin interfaces, plus distributed synchronization strategy—are not minor polish but essential architectural decisions that must be decomposed into explicit sub-FRs to ensure verifiability and risk mitigation. Decomposing now prevents late-stage interface rework and ensures safety compliance is baked into design, not bolted on. STOP would leave critical interface contracts and distributed consistency unspecified; REVISE_CURRENT_LEVEL would delay decomposition unnecessarily.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Ingest raw sensor telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems via standardized Telemetry interface contract (schema, rate/latency SLAs, error handling) without coupling NGHE to hardware specifics.

FR5.2: Detect and classify worksite features (obstacles, hazards, landmarks) from ingested telemetry using rule-based or learned classifiers, with pluggable conflict resolution to reconcile domain-specific constraints (vehicle kinematics, operator intent, sensor fusion heuristics).

FR5.3: Register detected features into a persistent, machine-readable worksite model with spatial indexing, versioning, and audit trail metadata (timestamp, operation type, user/system ID, change delta) for safety compliance.

FR5.4: Provide standardized Tasking query interface (spatial bounding box, feature type filters, temporal range, caching policy) to enable Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to retrieve features without tight coupling to NGHE internals.

FR5.5: Export audit trail and model change events to Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface contract (schema, versioning, compliance reporting format) to support worksite safety plan and operational policy compliance.

FR5.6: Synchronize worksite model across multiple NGHE nodes (if deployed) via distributed consensus or eventual consistency protocol, ensuring audit trail consistency and conflict resolution for concurrent feature updates.

revise.why: No revisions needed at current level. FR5, DP5, and IM (NGHE Solution) are sound. Decomposition into sub-FRs is the appropriate next step to formalize interface contracts and distributed system strategy.

assumptions: Telemetry interface is pull/push capable and can deliver raw sensor data at agreed rate/latency without NGHE needing to know Vehicle or Teleoperation hardware details.
Conflict resolution logic can be encapsulated as pluggable plugins within NGHE without leaking domain knowledge into Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.
Tasking query interface can support caching and lazy evaluation to prevent NGHE from becoming a synchronous bottleneck for real-time Vehicle/Teleoperation operations.
Safety Services can consume audit metadata via SafetyAlert interface without requiring direct access to NGHE model store internals.
Multi-node deployment (Network Link) is optional but must be designed for eventual consistency if required; single-node deployment is acceptable if distributed sync is deferred.
Audit trail schema can be versioned and extended without breaking existing Safety Services consumers.
open_risks: Telemetry interface contract not yet formalized: raw sensor data schema, ingestion rate/latency SLAs, and error handling (sensor dropout, malformed data) must be specified to prevent NGHE coupling to hardware.

Conflict resolution plugin interface not yet defined: domain-specific resolvers (vehicle kinematics, operator intent, sensor fusion heuristics) must be encapsulated to prevent logic diffusion into Vehicle/Teleoperation.

Tasking query interface latency SLAs not yet established: if NGHE becomes synchronous gatekeeper, real-time Vehicle/Teleoperation operations may be blocked; caching and asynchronous update strategies must be designed.

Distributed model synchronization strategy not yet specified: multi-node deployment may introduce consistency challenges (conflicting feature updates across nodes); consensus protocol or eventual consistency model must be documented.
Audit trail schema evolution not yet governed: Safety Services compliance requirements may drive schema changes; versioning and extensibility strategy must be established early.
Performance acceptance criteria not yet defined: feature detection, spatial registration, conflict resolution, and query response times must have explicit SLAs to prevent bottlenecks.
evidence_needed: Formalized Telemetry interface contract (schema, rate/latency SLAs, error handling) with Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.
Formalized Tasking query interface contract (query language, response schema, caching policy, latency SLAs) with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services consumers.
Formalized SafetyAlert audit interface contract with Safety Services (audit metadata schema, versioning strategy, compliance reporting format).
Conflict resolution plugin interface specification (registration, invocation, encapsulation guarantees).

Distributed model synchronization design (consensus protocol or eventual consistency model, conflict resolution for concurrent updates, audit trail consistency guarantees) if multi-node deployment is required.
Performance acceptance criteria (latency SLAs for feature detection, spatial registration, conflict resolution, query response times).
Audit trail retention policy, versioning granularity, and compliance reporting format aligned with 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' constraint.
Interface compliance tests (Telemetry schema validation, Tasking query contract validation, SafetyAlert audit schema validation).
Distributed system testing (if multi-node deployment is required) to validate consensus protocol and audit trail consistency.
Safety compliance review to confirm audit trail design meets regulatory and operational policy requirements.
